sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body:
CBSE Class 10 Maths Coordinate Geometry Notes:-Download PDF Here
Class 10 Maths Chapter 7 Coordinate Geometry Notes
The complete notes on coordinate geometry for Class 10 are provided here. Coordinate geometry teaches us the location of a point on a plane. For example, the coordinates of a point are (x, y), where x-coordinate (abscissa) denotes the distance of a point from the y-axis and y-coordinate (ordinate) denotes the distance of the point from the x-axis. Now we will learn to find the distance between two points and the area of a triangle using the distance formula when the coordinates of the points are given. Go through the below article and learn the points on the coordinate plane, distance formulas, section formulas and so on with a detailed explanation.
Students can refer to the short notes and MCQ questions along with a separate solution pdf of this chapter for quick revision from the links below:
- Coordinate Geometry Short Notes
- Coordinate Geometry MCQ Practice Questions
- Coordinate Geometry MCQ Practice Solutions
Basics of Coordinate Geometry
For more information on the Basics of Coordinate Geometry, watch the below video
To know more about Coordinate Geometry, vis…

- [NCERT solutions for Class 10 Maths Chapter 7 Coordinate Geometry](https://byjus.com/ncert-solutions-class-10-maths/chapter-7-coordinate-geometry/)
- [Coordinate Geometry Class 10 Notes Maths Chapter 7](https://www.learncbse.in/coordinate-geometry-class-10-notes/)

sections.title: 2. Key Definitions
sections.body:
**Definitions extracted from crawled pages:**

- The complete notes on coordinate geometry for Class 10 are provided here. Coordinate geometry teaches us the location of a point on a plane. For example, the coordinates of a point are (x, y), where x-coordinate (abscissa) denotes the distance of a point from the y-axis and y-coordinate (ordinate) denotes the distance of the point from the x-axis. Now we will learn to find the distance between two points and the area of a triangle using the distance formula when the coordinates of the points are given. Go through the below article and learn the points on the coordinate plane, distance formulas, section formulas and so on with a detailed explanation.
- A pair of numbers locate points on a plane called the coordinates. The distance of a point from the y-axis is known as abscissa or x-coordinate. The distance of a point from the x-axis is called ordinate or y-coordinate.
- Example: Consider a point P(3, 2), where 3 is the abscissa, and 2 is the ordinate. 3 represents the distance of point P from the y-axis, and 2 represents the distance of point P from the x-axis.
- The distance between two points that are on the same axis (x-axis or y-axis) is given by the difference between their ordinates if they are on the y-axis, else by the difference between their abscissa if they are on the x-axis.
- ΔPTQ is right-angled at T.
- Distance between any two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) is given by
- Where d is the distance between the points (x1,y1) and (x2,y2).
- If the point P(x, y) divides the line segment joining A(x1, y1) and B(x2, y2) internally in the ratio m:n, then, the coordinates of P are given by the section formula as:

sections.title: 3. Core Concepts
sections.body:
CBSE Class 10 Maths Coordinate Geometry Notes:-Download PDF Here
Class 10 Maths Chapter 7 Coordinate Geometry Notes
The complete notes on coordinate geometry for Class 10 are provided here. Coordinate geometry teaches us the location of a point on a plane. For example, the coordinates of a point are (x, y), where x-coordinate (abscissa) denotes the distance of a point from the y-axis and y-coordinate (ordinate) denotes the distance of the point from the x-axis. Now we will learn to find the distance between two points and the area of a triangle using the distance formula when the coordinates of the points are given. Go through the below article and learn the points on the coordinate plane, distance formulas, section formulas and so on with a detailed explanation.
Students can refer to the short notes and MCQ questions along with a separate solution pdf of this chapter for quick revision from the links below:
- Coordinate Geometry Short Notes
- Coordinate Geometry MCQ Practice Questions
- Coordinate Geometry MCQ Practice Solutions
Basics of Coordinate Geometry
For more information on the Basics of Coordinate Geometry, watch the below video
To know more about Coordinate Geometry, visit here.
Points on a Cartesian Plane
A pair of numbers locate points on a plane called the coordinates. The distance of a point from the y-axis is known as abscissa or x-coordinate. The distance of a point from the x-axis is called ordinate or y-coordinate.
Example: Consider a point P(3, 2), where 3 is the abscissa, and 2 is the ordinate. 3 represents the distance of point P from the y-axis, and 2 represents the distance of point P from the x-axis.
Distance Formula
Distance between Two Points on the Same Coordinate Axes
The distance between two points that are on the same axis (x-axis or y-axis) is given by the difference between their ordinates if they are on the y-axis, else by the difference between their abscissa if they are on the x-axis.
Distance AB = 6 – (-2) = 8 units
Distance CD =…

sections.title: 4. Formulas / Theorems
sections.body:
**Formulas / relations found:**

- Distance AB = 6 – (-2) = 8 units
- Distance CD = 4 – (-8) = 12 units
- PQ2 = PT2 + QT2 = (x2 – x1)2 + (y2 – y1)2
- PQ = √[x2 – x1)2 + (y2 – y1)2]
- d = √[x2 – x1)2+(y2 – y1)2]
- 4 = (3k-6)/(k+1)
- – 4k – 4 = 3k – 6
- k : 1 = 2 : 7

sections.title: 5. Solved Examples
sections.body:

For example, the coordinates of a point are (x, y), where x-coordinate (abscissa) denotes the distance of a point from the y-axis and y-coordinate (ordinate) denotes the distance of the point from the x-axis. Now we will learn to find the distance between two points and the area of a triangle using the distance formula when the coordinates of the points are given. - Example: Consider a point P(3, 2), where 3 is the abscissa, and 2 is the ordinate. 3 represents the distance of point P from the y-axis, and 2 represents the distance of point P from the x-axis.
- Example: Find the ratio when point (– 4, 6) divide the line segment joining the points A(– 6, 10) and B(3, – 8)?
- Example: What is the midpoint of line segment PQ whose coordinates are P (-3, 3) and Q (1, 4), respectively.
- Example: Find the coordinates of the points of trisection of the line segment joining the points A(2, – 2) and B(– 7, 4).
- Example: Find the coordinates of the centroid of a triangle whose vertices are given as (-1, -3), (2, 1) and (8, -4)

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Coordinate Geometry**._

**Practice questions from crawled sources:**

1. 1. Find the distance between the following pairs of points:
2. 2. Find the distance between the points (0, 0) and (36, 15). Can you now find the distance between the two towns, A and B, discussed in Section 7.2?
3. 3. Determine if the points (1, 5), (2, 3) and (-2, -11) are collinear.
4. 4. Check whether (5, – 2), (6, 4) and (7, – 2) are the vertices of an isosceles triangle.
5. 5. In a classroom, 4 friends are seated at points A, B, C and D, as shown in Fig. 7.8. Champa and Chameli walk into the class, and after observing for a few minutes, Champa asks Chameli, “Don’t you think ABCD is a square?” Chameli disagrees. Using the distance formula, find which of them is correct.
6. 6. Name the type of quadrilateral formed, if any, by the following points, and give reasons for your answer:
7. (i) Let the points (- 1, – 2), (1, 0), ( – 1, 2), and ( – 3, 0) represent the vertices A, B, C, and D of the given quadrilateral, respectively.
8. (ii) Let the points (- 3, 5), (3, 1), (0, 3), and (- 1, – 4) represent the vertices A, B, C, and D of the given quadrilateral, respectively.
9. (iii) Let the points (4, 5), (7, 6), (4, 3), and (1, 2) represent the vertices A, B, C, and D of the given quadrilateral, respectively.
10. 7. Find the point on the x-axis which is equidistant from (2, – 5) and (- 2, 9).

**Additional syllabus-based set** _(auto-generated for review — Phase-II ingest)_

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Distance formula** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Coordinate Geometry.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Distance formula** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Section formula** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Coordinate Geometry.

sections.title: 9. Real World Applications
sections.body:

- Exercise 7.1– 10 Questions, which includes 8 practical-based questions, 2 reasoning questions
- Exercise 7.3– 5 Questions, which includes 3 long answer questions, 2 practical-based questions

- This chapter deals with finding the area between two points whose coordinate values are provided. For instance, the area of a triangle. This chapter has some basic concepts like the area of a triangle, rhombus, the distance between sides, and intersections. This chapter teaches you the relationship between numerical and geometry and their application in our daily lives.
